question: Are there any quota / reserved seats?
answer: There are no quota seats.
answer_html: There are no quota seats.

question: Are there any reserved / quota seats for admission in MBBS Programme?
answer: Admissions in NSHS will be purely on merit , based upon the MDCAT conducted by NUMS .
answer_html: Admissions in NSHS will be purely on merit, based upon the MDCAT conducted by NUMS.

question: Can a candidate apply both for open merit and ACT based seats?
answer: Yes, on the basis of NET / MDCAT Score and required ACT Score.
answer_html: Yes, on the basis of NET / MDCAT Score and required ACT Score.

question: Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes?
answer: Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission. Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process.
answer_html:
Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission.

Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process.

question: Can a candidate with FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification, appeared in additional mathematics paper and waiting for the result, apply for admission in Engineering programs on the basis of Pre Medical marks?
answer: Candidate can apply on the basis of Pre-Medical result, however he / she has to provide the additional math result with passing marks and overall 60% marks in HSSC at the time of joining NUST.
answer_html: Candidate can apply on the basis of Pre-Medical result, however he / she has to provide the additional math result with passing marks and overall 60% marks in HSSC at the time of joining NUST.

question: Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Computer Science?
answer: Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme.
answer_html: Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme.

question: Can foreign / international students apply for admission in BSHND programme?
answer: Foreign students are eligible to apply on the basis of NET-Applied Sciences / NUMS-MDCAT / National MDCAT / ACT Test.
answer_html: Foreign students are eligible to apply on the basis of NET-Applied Sciences / NUMS-MDCAT / National MDCAT / ACT Test.

question: Can foreign students apply for admission at NSHS?
answer: Foreign students can apply to MBBS Programme at NSHS on the basis of NUMS MDCAT, SAT-II or foreign MCAT / UCAT score.
answer_html: Foreign students can apply to MBBS Programme at NSHS on the basis of NUMS MDCAT, SAT-II or foreign MCAT / UCAT score.

question: Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Humanities group?
answer: Yes, any candidate who has Mathematics as one of the mandatory subjects in HSSC / A level / Equivalent, is eligible to apply for computing programmes.
answer_html: Yes, any candidate who has Mathematics as one of the mandatory subjects in HSSC / A level / Equivalent, is eligible to apply for computing programmes.

question: Can I apply for more than one programme?
answer: Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria.
answer_html: Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria.

question: Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result?
answer: Re-checking of Paper Based Entry Test may be requested within 5 days of the declaration of result along with a fee of Rs.500/- in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST. The original bank draft / pay order should be dispatched to Admissions Directorate, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad, along with written application. Re-checking involves the verification of paper for any unmarked answers and error free totaling of marks.
answer_html: Re-checking of Paper Based Entry Test may be requested within 5 days of the declaration of result along with a fee of Rs.500/- in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST. The original bank draft / pay order should be dispatched to Admissions Directorate, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad, along with written application. Re-checking involves the verification of paper for any unmarked answers and error free totaling of marks.

question: Can I get a copy of NUST Entry Test result?

answer_html: Desirous candidates may obtain the result details of their Test from the link available on NUST website.

question: How can I submit the application processing fee (online) using 1Link option?
answer: Candidates can use the unique 17-digit 1Bill Invoice / Consumer No. mentioned under the Fee Invoice section , to pay their fee via 1Link service through any of the following payment modes: Mobile App / Online Banking (Any Bank) ATM (Any Bank) Easy Paisa / Jazz Cash etc. The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don’t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly.
answer_html:
Candidates can use the unique 17-digit 1Bill Invoice / Consumer No. mentioned under the Fee Invoice section, to pay their fee via 1Link service through any of the following payment modes:
  • Mobile App / Online Banking (Any Bank)
  • ATM (Any Bank)
  • Easy Paisa / Jazz Cash etc.
The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don’t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly.

question: How can I submit the application processing fee?
answer: Candidates can submit the fee through any of the following options :- 1Link (1Bill Invoice Number) Credit Card

  • Credit Card

question: How many allied programmes does NSHS offer?
answer: Currently, NSHS is offering admission to BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND).
answer_html: Currently, NSHS is offering admission to BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND).

question: I am a born Pakistani with a foreign passport; can I apply as an expatriate student?
answer: If you don't have Pakistani nationality like having CNIC / NICOP, you can apply as an international student (foreign student). You can apply on the basis of NET or ACT/SAT or both as per eligibility criteria but have to pay tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for foreign nationals. If you are a POC holder and applying on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT national seat, you will be paying tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for national student.
answer_html:
If you don't have Pakistani nationality like having CNIC / NICOP, you can apply as an international student (foreign student). You can apply on the basis of NET or ACT/SAT or both as per eligibility criteria but have to pay tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for foreign nationals.

If you are a POC holder and applying on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT national seat, you will be paying tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for national student.

question: I am a foreigner and want to get admission at NUST?
answer: If you fulfill the eligibility criteria, you can apply for admission on the basis of NET or ACT / SAT (International seat) or both as per policy in vogue. For details, visit https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/ .
answer_html:
If you fulfill the eligibility criteria, you can apply for admission on the basis of NET or ACT / SAT (International seat) or both as per policy in vogue.
For details, visit https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/.

question: I am an overseas Pakistani who has completed O & A level from abroad, do I have to pass Urdu, Islamiyat / Ethics, and Pakistan Studies to get IBCC equivalence?
answer: No, there isn’t any such compulsory requirement of passing the above-mentioned subjects, as per IBCC equivalence rules. For further details, please visit IBCC website: https://ibcc.edu.pk/rules-for-equivalence/
answer_html: No, there isn’t any such compulsory requirement of passing the above-mentioned subjects, as per IBCC equivalence rules. For further details, please visit IBCC website: https://ibcc.edu.pk/rules-for-equivalence/

question: I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024. Can I apply?
answer: Yes, you can apply, as in such case you will be evaluated on the basis of ‘O’ levels equivalence marks but you have to provide equivalence certificate of ‘A’ level within 15 days of announcement of result or at the time of admission.
answer_html: Yes, you can apply, as in such case you will be evaluated on the basis of ‘O’ levels equivalence marks but you have to provide equivalence certificate of ‘A’ level within 15 days of announcement of result or at the time of admission.

question: I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day?
answer: NUST is conducting NET in paper based format on one day and in computer based format in two sessions daily, on different days, as per the schedule. As per policy, if a candidate misses the Test session he / she is not given another chance. But in case of extreme emergency, requests may be entertained on case to case basis, depending on availability of space.
answer_html: NUST is conducting NET in paper based format on one day and in computer based format in two sessions daily, on different days, as per the schedule. As per policy, if a candidate misses the Test session he / she is not given another chance. But in case of extreme emergency, requests may be entertained on case to case basis, depending on availability of space.

question: I have DAE background. Which programmes can I apply for?
answer: DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE The detail of Engineering programmes offered to DAE candidates as approved by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) can be accessed by visiting https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/ .
answer_html:
1. DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE
  2. The detail of Engineering programmes offered to DAE candidates as approved by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) can be accessed by visiting https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/.

question: If I am selected on all 3 merits, what should I do?
answer: If selected on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT on national as well as international seat, you have the option to retain any one seat of your own choice.
answer_html: If selected on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT on national as well as international seat, you have the option to retain any one seat of your own choice.

question: If I have taken ACT / SAT Tests, do I still have to sit in the Entry Test?
answer: Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for applying on the basis of ACT / SAT and NET. Those applying on ACT / SAT basis does not require to appear in NET, however they can opt for NET in addition to ACT /SAT specified seats. In this way, they will be considered for both the streams.
answer_html: Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for applying on the basis of ACT / SAT and NET. Those applying on ACT / SAT basis does not require to appear in NET, however they can opt for NET in addition to ACT /SAT specified seats. In this way, they will be considered for both the streams.

question: Is a candidate having less than 60% marks in FSc part-1 eligible to apply for admission in NUST?
answer: Yes, the marks entered for FSc part-1 will be utilized for merit generation. However on joining NUST, the candidate must have secure 60% or more marks in FSc complete result.
answer_html: Yes, the marks entered for FSc part-1 will be utilized for merit generation. However on joining NUST, the candidate must have secure 60% or more marks in FSc complete result.

question: Is an equivalence certificate of qualification other than Matric & FSc / FA necessary? Can I not submit my O'level and A' level certificates? (I ask so, because I will be applying from abroad)?
answer: Equivalence certificates duly issued by IBCC Pakistan are necessary for those candidates who do not possess Matric & FSc / FA qualifications. Submission of only O&A level grade / mark sheets will not serve the purpose. All non FSc stream candidates must have equivalence certificate of their qualification, duly obtained from IBCC, Pakistan in relevant groups/subjects and with minimum 60% marks.
answer_html: Equivalence certificates duly issued by IBCC Pakistan are necessary for those candidates who do not possess Matric & FSc / FA qualifications. Submission of only O&A level grade / mark sheets will not serve the purpose. All non FSc stream candidates must have equivalence certificate of their qualification, duly obtained from IBCC, Pakistan in relevant groups/subjects and with minimum 60% marks.

question: Is migration to NUST allowed?
answer: Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. However, applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered for migration to NUST. For detailed migration policy click here .
answer_html: Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. However, applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered for migration to NUST. For detailed migration policy click here.

question: Is there any age limit for Undergraduate Admission?
answer: There is no age limit for Undergraduate Admission.
answer_html: There is no age limit for Undergraduate Admission.

question: Is there any installment plan for the payment of tuition fee?
answer: As per PM&DC Regulations, a student can pay the tuition fee in installments on a quarterly , six monthly or annual basis , with a discount of 0% on quarterly, 2% on six monthly and 4% on annual basis. Necessary details will be incorporated in the fee invoice.
answer_html: As per PM&DC Regulations, a student can pay the tuition fee in installments on a quarterly, six monthly or annual basis, with a discount of 0% on quarterly, 2% on six monthly and 4% on annual basis. Necessary details will be incorporated in the fee invoice.

question: Is there any negative marking in the NUST Entry Test?
answer: No, there is no negative marking.
answer_html: No, there is no negative marking.

question: Name on my CNIC and Passport is different from that on my academic documents (O / A Level) or equivalence certificate. Which name should I use to apply for admission?
answer: ​For this, you have to get your name changed on your CNIC and Passport, because University will use the name as per your Matric or O level equivalence certificate (SSC), duly issued by IBCC.
answer_html: ​For this, you have to get your name changed on your CNIC and Passport, because University will use the name as per your Matric or O level equivalence certificate (SSC), duly issued by IBCC.

question: What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT?
answer: Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students. For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:- Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system. Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad. Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board.
answer_html:
Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students. For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:-
  • Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system.
  • Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad.
  • Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board.

question: What documents are essentially required to be submitted while applying for admission?
answer: While applying for admission, possession of Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) / Form 'B' or NICOP is mandatory. Online registration system does not require attachment of documents / credentials. However, the same will be required for verification during selection and admission process of selected candidates.
answer_html: While applying for admission, possession of Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) / Form 'B' or NICOP is mandatory. Online registration system does not require attachment of documents / credentials. However, the same will be required for verification during selection and admission process of selected candidates.

question: What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates?
answer: Gap year / repeater candidates are being considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit.
answer_html: Gap year / repeater candidates are being considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit.

question: What is the application processing fee for online registration for admission?
answer: Application processing fee will be charged according to the following structure: NUST Entry Test (per exam) Pakistani / Dual Nationality Holder - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- Foreign National (Non Pakistani) - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- For ACT / SAT Candidates ACT / SAT based National seat - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- ACT / SAT based International seat - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- Application processing fee is neither refundable nor transferable . It will remain valid only for the series for which it has been paid.
answer_html:
Application processing fee will be charged according to the following structure:
  • NUST Entry Test (per exam)  
    • Pakistani / Dual Nationality Holder   -   Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/-
    • Foreign National (Non Pakistani)       -   Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/-
  • For ACT / SAT Candidates
    • ACT / SAT based National seat            -   Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/-
    • ACT / SAT based International seat    -   Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/-
Application processing fee is neither refundable nor transferable. It will remain valid only for the series for which it has been paid.

question: What is the deadline for submission of ACT / SAT score?
answer: Last date for ACT / SAT score to reach NUST directly from College Board, USA, is 25 July, 2025. NUST institutional code to receive ACT scores is 2915 and of SAT scores is 2790. ACT / SAT scores are valid for two years only. It is the responsibility of candidates to ensure the provision of ACT / SAT scores to Undergraduate Section, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad by the given deadline.
answer_html:
Last date for ACT / SAT score to reach NUST directly from College Board, USA, is 25 July, 2025.
NUST institutional code to receive ACT scores is 2915 and of SAT scores is 2790. ACT / SAT scores are valid for two years only.
It is the responsibility of candidates to ensure the provision of ACT / SAT scores to Undergraduate Section, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad by the given deadline.

question: What is the merit generation criteria for BSHND programme?
answer: Merit details are as under: NET / MDCAT / ACT Test * - 75% HSSC (Pre-Medical) / A level / Equivalent - 15% SSC / O level / Equivalent - 10% * For ACT Based International Seats, 100% weightage will be given to required ACT Score.
answer_html:
Merit details are as under:
  • NET / MDCAT / ACT Test*                               -    75%
  • HSSC (Pre-Medical) / A level / Equivalent       -   15%
  • SSC / O level / Equivalent                                -   10%
* For ACT Based International Seats, 100% weightage will be given to required ACT Score.

Grasping the concepts available in different textbooks will be beneficial. https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings
answer_html:
NUST Entry Test is primarily based on SSC / HSSC level studies (Part-1 & Part-II) and questions are in the form of MCQs with four answer options each. The paper of English is on the pattern of SAT exam. Sample Test.

question: Which admission tests are acceptable for admission in BSHND programme?
answer: For Open Merit Seats NET-Applied Sciences NUMS MDCAT National MDCAT For ACT Based Seats ACT Test

For Open Merit Seats
  • NET-Applied Sciences

question: Which MDCAT will be valid for admission in NSHS?
answer: You can apply for admission on the basis of NUMS MDCAT only.
answer_html: You can apply for admission on the basis of NUMS MDCAT only.

question: Who is exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee?
answer: First, second and third position holders in HSSC exams of different BISEs are exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee. Such candidates should contact NUST through email at ugadmissions@nust.edu.pk along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board.
answer_html: First, second and third position holders in HSSC exams of different BISEs are exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee. Such candidates should contact NUST through email at ugadmissions@nust.edu.pk along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board.

question: Whom should I contact for queries about NUST Entry Test for Undergraduate Programmes?
answer: You can contact through Phone: +92 51-90856878 e-mail: ugadmissions@nust.edu.pk.
answer_html: You can contact through Phone: +92 51-90856878 e-mail: ugadmissions@nust.edu.pk.

question: Will the admission be confirmed in case of quarterly or six-monthly payment of tuition fee?
answer: The student will be admitted to the MBBS programme on payment of tuition fee in quarterly or six-monthly installments.
answer_html: The student will be admitted to the MBBS programme on payment of tuition fee in quarterly or six-monthly installments.

question: Will there be hostel facility available for the MBBS students?
answer: The facility will be available both for boys and girls as per the NUST SoP. The students are requested to apply for the same on confirmation of selection. For the details regarding hostel charges and to apply online, visit the following links: https://campuslife.nust.edu.pk/facility-and-amenity/housing-and-dining/
answer_html: The facility will be available both for boys and girls as per the NUST SoP. The students are requested to apply for the same on confirmation of selection. For the details regarding hostel charges and to apply online, visit the following links: https://campuslife.nust.edu.pk/facility-and-amenity/housing-and-dining/

question: Would the admission processing fee / security deposit be refundable in case a student does not join the University?
answer: The university will refund only the security deposit to the candidates who do not join the university. Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable.
answer_html: The university will refund only the security deposit to the candidates who do not join the university.  Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable.
